# 2026-10-17

- 6 miles, Crestwood South loop, easy

## Dad
- Three years today. Carried the whistle on the run.
- Visited Greenwood Memorial, then dinner at Mom's.

- 5 miles before clinic

- Devon's block starts looking sharper

- Rest day, no run
- Cooked with Mom and Keisha

## Gratitude
- A full table

# 2026-12-14

## Birthday
- 32. Long run, then Sunday dinner moved to today.

## Gratitude
- The team. Mom. Keisha and the kids.

# 2027-01-15

## MLK Day
- Civil rights commemoration downtown with the men's choir

## Run
- 5 easy miles, cold morning

# 2027-02-06

## Mercedes-Benz Marathon weekend
- Ran the half. 1:38, negative split the back five.
- Mom and Keisha at the finish on 20th.

# 2027-02-13

## Season opener
- First meet of the season
- Checked every athlete's injury status this morning

## Run
- 4 easy shakeout miles

# 2027-03-01

## Run
- 7 miles, tempo middle 3

## Open
- Finalize Devon's visit follow-ups
- Update base-mileage plan

# 2027-03-15

## Run
- 8 miles long, steady

## Coaching
- Relay handoff work all week
- Confirm meet entries

# ACL Return-to-Sport Protocol

Phased criteria-based return for [[devon-recruiting]] caliber athletes.

## Phase 1 — Restore
- Full knee extension, quad set with good VMO
- Stationary bike, swelling under control

## Phase 2 — Strength
- Double-leg then single-leg press
- Limb symmetry index target > 80%

## Phase 3 — Power
- Double-leg to single-leg hops
- Decelerate and cut drills

## Clearance
- LSI > 90%, single-leg hop battery, no apprehension

# Sprint Mechanics Drills

## Acceleration
- Wall drives, 3x10s
- Sled marches, 4x20m
- Block starts, 6x10m on the whistle

## Top speed
- A-skips and B-skips, 2x20m
- Wickets, 6x6 hurdles
- Flying 20s, 4 reps full recovery

## Cues
- Push the ground back, don't reach
- Tall posture by 30m

# Block Start Checklist

## Set-up
- Front block two shoe-lengths from line
- Rear block three shoe-lengths
- Hands shoulder-width, fingers bridged

## Set
- Hips above shoulders
- Shin angles ~45 / ~90

## Go
- Drive both arms, punch the lead knee
- First three steps low and long

# Relay Handoff Notes

## Exchange zones
- 4x100 blind pass, push exchange
- Mark go-step at 18 feet for incoming

## Drills
- Walk-through then build to race pace
- Same hand all week, no swapping

## 4x400
- Visual pass, slow into the line, accelerate out

# Devon Mitchell Recruiting

## Status
- Senior, 100m 10.6h / 200m 21.4h
- Qualifying GPA, NCAA eligibility center registered

## Contacts
- Auburn — Brian Tolliver, called Oct
- Alabama — Renee Dawson, sent film
- South Alabama — Sam Whitlow, interested

## Next
- Unofficial visit Dec 5, I'm driving
- Update highlight reel after season opener

# 2026-27 Meet Schedule

## Indoor
- Birmingham CrossPlex developmental meets

## Outdoor
- Feb 13 — Crestwood season opener
- Relays and area qualifiers to follow

## Notes
- Submit entries the Monday before each meet
- Confirm transportation with Pat Hendricks

# Injury Status Board

## Out
- Kayla Simmons — hamstring strain, Phase 2 rehab

## Limited
- Marcus Reed — shin splints, pool running

## Full go
- Devon Mitchell
- Tre Donaldson
- Brandon Settles

Update every Monday before practice.

# Sunday Dinner Rotation

## Standing
- 9:30 service at New Hope AME
- 2:00 dinner at Mom's

## This month's menu
- Smothered chicken, greens, mac, cornbread
- Keisha brings dessert

## Notes
- 1st and 3rd Sundays I'm at men's choir 9:00

# Team Meal Prep

## Pre-meet plate
- Rice, grilled chicken, roasted vegetables
- Bananas and water, nothing fried the night before

## Recovery
- Chocolate milk within 30 minutes
- Protein and carbs, hydrate through the evening

## Shopping
- Publix run Saturday, split the cost with boosters

# Running Log — Winter Base

## Weekly target
- 40-45 miles, one long, one tempo

## Standing
- Weekday 5:15 AM Crestwood South loop
- Saturday 7:00 AM long run, 8-10 mi

## Recent
- 2027-03-15: 8 mi long, steady
- 2027-03-01: 7 mi, tempo middle 3

# Half Marathon Plan

Mercedes-Benz Marathon weekend, running the half.

## Build
- Long runs to 11 miles by late January
- One tempo, one workout, easy rest

## Race week
- Cut volume, hold intensity short
- Lay out gear and bib the night before

## Goal
- Sub 1:40, even effort, finish on 20th Street

# Booster 5K Fundraiser

Iron City Sports Medicine charity 5K, raising for team travel.

## To do
- Order bibs and timing chips
- Line up volunteers from New Hope AME
- Set the course on the Crestwood South loop

## Sponsors
- Reach out to local businesses, ask Dr. Lawson for clinic support

# Patient Caseload Notes

Iron City Sports Medicine, Mon-Fri treatment block 7:00 AM-1:30 PM.

## Active
- Post-op rotator cuff, week 6, scapular control
- Ankle sprain, return-to-run progression
- Patellar tendinopathy, isometric loading

## Reminders
- Chart same day with Monica at front desk
- Flag any red flags to Dr. Lawson

# Continuing Education Log

## SCS recertification
- Renewal due, register for the course this winter
- Track CEUs in the binder

## Recent reading
- Tendon loading review
- Hamstring rehab criteria progression

## Notes
- License PT-4738 current, keep CPR card active

# Inbox — quick capture

- Ask Demetrius about Tuesday distance group warm-up
- Confirm bus reservation for next invite
- Pick up new whistle and stopwatch batteries
- Text Devon's mom about the visit paperwork
